task_ref.title: Stabilize cache client dependency before release cut

decision: Pin the synthetic acme-cache-client dependency to 4.2.7 and defer the v4.3 config migration.
problem_context: A minor upgrade to the synthetic acme-cache-client dependency introduced a required tls.ca_bundle setting. The current service config does not provide it, which caused startup failure in staging and blocked the release branch.

alternatives_considered.name: Complete the v4.3 config migration immediately
alternatives_considered.reason: Would require a wider deployment and secret-distribution change late in the release window.

alternatives_considered.name: Pin acme-cache-client to 4.2.7
alternatives_considered.reason: Restores the previous config contract with the smallest safe patch.

alternatives_considered.name: Disable TLS verification temporarily
alternatives_considered.reason: Would weaken transport safety and conflict with the repository security baseline.

selected_option.name: Pin acme-cache-client to 4.2.7
selected_option.summary: Replace the caret dependency range with an exact pin and keep the current cache TLS configuration unchanged for this release.

rationale: Pinning the dependency removes the immediate regression with the smallest bounded change, while keeping the eventual config migration visible as explicit follow-up work.

evidence_refs.summary: Synthetic upgrade note states that verified TLS connections now require tls.ca_bundle.
Current synthetic cache config defines tls.enabled and server_name but not ca_bundle.
The compatibility test failed on v4.3 and passed again after pinning 4.2.7.

validation_run.summary: Synthetic cache client compatibility tests passed with the pinned dependency.
The service build completed without requiring additional config changes.

remaining_risks: The eventual v4.3 migration is still pending and must add explicit tls.ca_bundle handling.
follow_up: Add the new TLS config fields in a later change and retry the v4.3 upgrade in staging.
